template_name: Standard Prescription Format – Breast Masses, Vaginal Bleeding, and Other Genital Anomalies in Neonates
condition: Breast Masses, Vaginal Bleeding, and Other Genital Anomalies

red_flag_genital_findings.clues: Salt loss, pigmentation, atypical genital anatomy

history_points: Consanguinity or previous sibling with CAH / DSD
Duration and amount of vaginal bleeding
Any breast manipulation or squeezing by caregivers

medicines.name: No medicine required
medicines.indication: Physiological breast swelling, mucoid discharge, first-week vaginal bleeding

supportive_care: Do not squeeze or manipulate breasts
Monitor duration of bleeding or discharge
Observe for fever, redness, tenderness, swelling

ambiguous_genitalia: Refer to pediatric endocrinology / higher center

parent_counseling: These findings are common and usually hormonal / physiological
Breast swelling or milky discharge often resolves spontaneously
Do not squeeze or massage the breasts
First-week vaginal bleeding is usually self-limiting
Maintain gentle hygiene only; no internal cleaning
Seek review if bleeding persists, discharge is foul-smelling, breast becomes red/tender, or genitalia appear unclear
opd_checklist: Breast enlargement warm? red? discharge? asymmetric?
Signs of virilization or salt loss?

follow_up.routine_follow_up: 3–7 days or earlier if symptoms persist
follow_up.urgent_follow_up: Immediately if fever, breast redness, persistent bleeding, ambiguous genitalia, or hard inguinal swelling develops

referral_indications: Hard or non-reducible inguinal / labial mass
Salt-loss symptoms or suspicion of CAH
diagnosis_name: Breast Masses, Vaginal Bleeding and Other Genital Anomalies in Neonates
counselling: These findings are common and usually hormonal / physiological — ये लक्षण आम हैं और आमतौर पर हार्मोन के कारण होते हैं
Breast swelling or milky discharge often resolves spontaneously — स्तन की सूजन या दूध जैसा रिसाव अपने आप ठीक हो जाता है
Do not squeeze or massage the breasts — स्तनों को दबाएं या मालिश न करें
First-week vaginal bleeding is usually self-limiting — पहले हफ्ते में योनि से खून आना आमतौर पर अपने आप रुक जाता है
Maintain gentle hygiene only; no internal cleaning — सिर्फ़ हल्की सफ़ाई रखें; अंदर की सफ़ाई न करें

Seek review if bleeding persists, discharge is foul-smelling, breast becomes red/tender, or genitalia appear unclear — अगर खून बंद न हो, बदबूदार रिसाव हो, स्तन लाल/दर्दनाक हो, या जननांग स्पष्ट न दिखें तो डॉक्टर को दिखाएं
warning_signs: Persistent vaginal bleeding beyond first week — पहले हफ्ते के बाद भी योनि से खून आना
Breast redness, warmth or pus — स्तन पर लालिमा, गर्मी या मवाद
Fever or poor feeding — बुखार या कम दूध पीना
Inguinal swelling that does not reduce — जांघ की सूजन जो कम न हो
Signs of salt loss: vomiting, poor weight gain, dehydration — नमक की कमी के संकेत: उल्टी, वज़न न बढ़ना, पानी की कमी
expected_course: Neonatal breast enlargement resolves within 2-4 weeks as maternal hormones clear. Witch milk (galactorrhea) may persist for up to 2 months. First-week vaginal bleeding (estrogen withdrawal) resolves within 3-5 days. Mucoid vaginal discharge may persist for 2-4 weeks. All are self-limiting and benign.
key_clinical_points: Neonatal breast enlargement is due to transplacental maternal estrogen — never squeeze
Squeezing breasts can cause mastitis/abscess — educate caregivers strongly
Vaginal bleeding in first week is estrogen withdrawal — benign if small amount
Always examine for ambiguous genitalia — rule out CAH (check 17-OHP, electrolytes)
Imperforate hymen presents as bulging introitus — needs surgical referral
Breast abscess requires antibiotics and possibly drainage
Salt-losing CAH is a neonatal emergency — vomiting, dehydration, hyperkalemia

severity_assessment.mild: Bilateral breast swelling, clear/milky nipple discharge, small amount vaginal bleeding in first week — reassurance only
severity_assessment.moderate: Unilateral tender breast, persistent bleeding, prominent clitoris — investigation (CBC, 17-OHP, electrolytes), close follow-up
severity_assessment.severe: Breast abscess, ambiguous genitalia, salt loss symptoms, hard inguinal mass — urgent evaluation, specialist referral, possible NICU admission
